week3.practice1_html:
What it is

“Win Your Day with Breakfast” helps you start your morning with a strong foundation — a quick, protein-centered meal that steadies energy, mood, and scalp health right from the first bite.

Why this practice matters for you now

Teresa, you’re in the stabilization phase after years of stress‑linked shedding. Adding a high‑protein breakfast is your “big domino” because steady morning fuel calms stress hormones that have been tugging at your follicles. One balanced plate each morning gives your body the signal that safety and recovery have begun — helping you move from constant shedding toward healthy regrowth.

Step-by-step implementation

  1. Pick your protein. Aim for 25–30 g: for omnivores, try two eggs with Greek yogurt; for plant-based, go for tofu scramble or a smoothie with pea protein and nut butter.
  2. Add colour. Include a handful of berries or spinach to bring antioxidants that soothe your scalp’s micro‑inflammation.
  3. Balance with healthy fats. Sprinkle seeds or half an avocado — they help your body absorb fat‑soluble vitamins crucial for hair anchoring.
  4. Prep ahead. Batch-boil eggs, blend smoothie packs, or cook tofu cubes on Sunday so breakfast takes under five minutes each morning.
  5. Eat within an hour of waking. This reinforces circadian stability — important since your bedtime shifts by over an hour across the week.

What to look for

  • Early wins: Morning energy steadies; mid‑day sugar or caffeine cravings fade; scalp feels less oily or reactive within two weeks.
  • Watch‑outs: Doom‑scrolling while eating can blunt stress‑relief benefits — instead, sit by a sunny window, breathe, and actually taste your food.
  • Consistency builds momentum — and your follicles notice. One calm, protein‑powered morning at a time. 🌱
week3.practice2_html:
What it is

Tweak What’s on Your Plate helps you subtly upgrade what’s already in your kitchen so your meals consistently deliver the protein, minerals, and healthy fats your follicles crave. It’s not a diet—it’s nourishment designed for growth.

Why this practice matters for you now

Teresa, your shedding pattern shows you’re in the stabilization phase: follicles are still sensitive from years of emotional strain, but steadier nutrition now is your bridge toward regrowth. Since you’re only hitting one high‑protein meal a day, adding steady protein through the day is your “big domino.” It stabilizes stress hormones and keeps the growth cycle from slipping backward. Each balanced plate helps signal safety to your body again. 🌿

Step-by-step implementation

  1. Anchor breakfast with protein (20–30 g). Think scramble with two eggs + smoked salmon, or Greek yogurt topped with seeds and berries.
  2. At lunch and dinner, fill one-quarter of your plate with lean protein—chicken, lentils, tofu, fish, or quinoa/beans combo.
  3. Add colorful plants and healthy fats: olive oil drizzle, half an avocado, or a small handful of nuts; these calm inflammation and steady mood.
  4. Hydrate and slow down—pause your phone, breathe, taste. This tells your nervous system you’re safe to digest and absorb.

What to look for

  • Early wins: steadier energy after meals, fewer cravings or mood dips, and slightly calmer shedding within 10–14 days.
  • Watch‑outs: your tendency to decompress by doom scrolling can lead to late-night snacking or skipped meals; set your phone aside during dinner to protect that rhythm.
  • Consistency builds momentum—one balanced plate at a time, Teresa. Your body feels every signal of stability you send it. 💛
week3.practice3_html:
What it is

Calm Your Scalp and Body is a grounding ritual that helps your nervous system shift from “fight or flight” into a steady, safe state — the zone where your follicles can finally breathe and recover.

Why this practice matters for you now

Teresa, after years of relationship stress and ongoing worry, your body still lives in alert mode even when the day is over. In this stabilization phase, calming your system is your big domino — it tells your scalp, “You’re safe now.” Each time you unwind before bedtime, you lay the foundation for shedding to settle and regrowth to begin within the next few months.

Step-by-step implementation

  1. Find your cue: Each evening, choose one fixed moment (like brushing teeth or plugging in your phone) that signals your five-minute calm-down window.
  2. Breathe to reset: Sit comfortably, place one hand on your chest and the other at your nape, and breathe in for 4 counts, out for 6. Feel your scalp soften beneath your palm.
  3. Soothing touch: Gently massage the sides of your head from temples toward the crown; use slow circular motions to relieve tightness.
  4. Digital boundary: When the urge to doom scroll hits, flip the phone facedown and play a calming sound instead — signals safety faster than any screen.
  5. Anchor with gratitude: Whisper one thing your body handled well today. This quiet acknowledgment rewires tension into trust.

What to look for

  • Early wins: Falling asleep faster, waking with a looser scalp feeling, and fewer frantic thoughts in the evenings within 10–14 days.
  • Watch-outs: Late-night scrolling can reignite stress; replace the screen with music or a light stretch before bed.
  • Consistency builds momentum — one calm breath, one grounded night at a time. 🌱

week3.practice3_title: Practice 3: Calm Your Scalp and Body
week3.week_summary_html:
Finding Calm So Your Follicles Can Catch Their Breath 🌿

Teresa, you’re in the stabilization phase — that delicate space where shedding begins to ease once your system feels safe again. Because your main trigger is ongoing emotional stress from relationship strain, this week’s focus is on helping your body downshift from constant alert back into repair mode.

Each of this week’s practices — your sleep anchor, wind‑down stack, and alcohol pause — work together to quiet cortisol and reset your nightly rhythm. Even with 7–8 hours in bed, your variable bedtime and frequent evening drinks keep stress hormones active longer. When you create a predictable wind‑down and give your liver a break, your body finally gets the signal: it’s safe to turn off shedding and start restoring growth signals.

  • 🧠 Cortisol reset: lowers the chronic stress signal that keeps follicles in “pause.”
  • ⚖️ Hormone rhythm: steadier evenings mean balanced estrogen and thyroid cues for growth.
  • 💤 Deep‑sleep repair: boosts nighttime growth hormone that rebuilds weakened follicles.
  • 🌱 Emotional safety: calmer nights teach your body that the danger is over — paving the way for visible regrowth.

Every evening you choose calm over scrolling or a drink, you’re teaching your body to let go of stress and hold on to your hair 🌱.
